response.justification: The candidate is a capable, versatile senior/staff-level individual contributor with genuine distributed-systems depth and recent exposure to AI-agent tooling, but the role is an executive CTO position with hard requirements he does not meet: there is no evidence of senior technical leadership, no track record scaling engineering teams from 10 to 30+ or developing a leadership layer, no production ownership of multi-agent AI systems (only contributions to MCP generators and agent tooling), and no experience operating AI systems in regulated environments or governing outsourced delivery partners. His fintech exposure is limited to roughly 15 months as a senior developer at RIDE Capital. Strong engineer, wrong altitude for this role.
strengths: Deep backend and low-latency distributed-systems experience, including microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Recent hands-on work on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at Naver, showing current relevance to agentic workflows
Broad full-stack range (web, mobile, embedded, DevOps) with strong software-craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) and some fintech exposure at RIDE Capital
concerns: No engineering-leadership or executive experience — no evidence of managing, hiring, or scaling teams, developing technical leaders, or board-level communication
No demonstrated production experience building and operating multi-agent AI systems; agentic work appears limited to tooling contributions rather than owning stateful agent architectures
No experience operating AI in regulated/high-stakes financial environments, designing credit-risk or compliance infrastructure, or governing hybrid/outsourced engineering models

key_factors.factor: No senior leadership or team-scaling track record
Missing production multi-agent AI system ownership
Strong distributed-systems and full-stack engineering depth
